Cyber/Operational Incident Summary — Horizon Oasis Utilities PJSC (NE: HOUC) — 2026-06-05
This memo primarily documents a tabletop/training run and operational hygiene; it is not a trading signal and references PDPL controls for awareness.
On 2026-06-03 21:40 AST, SOC telemetry flagged unauthorized queries on the pre-release ESG emissions dashboard for HOUC within the issuer staging network; this information is not public and may be material under Kingdom Markets Authority market-conduct rules pending assessment.
We have paused the NE Issuer Portal ESG upload workflow and invoked a temporary internal blackout for the sustainability reporting workstream only; trading by the wider company is unaffected.
Forensic review shows no extraction of customer records; two contractor rows displayed masked iqama values (IQ-77***2*1) and a test Commercial Registration field “CR-10X012345” (invalid), with no unmasked identifiers stored.
PDPL assessment indicates a low-risk event; a DPIA addendum will be logged, and any cross-border log mirroring will follow the SDAIA transfer register process via the national data authority gateway.
Third-party “Falaj Digital Custody LLC” SFTP endpoint was used only for synthetic ESG payloads in a sandbox; the term “crypto wallet” in related training refers to loyalty token custody and is not an investment product.
Staff will attend “Information Barriers 101” and “Insider-List Hygiene” refreshers; the slides are generic and do not include issuer-sensitive content or deal codenames.
The climate-risk explainer deck prepared for our website describes public scenario methodologies and stale 2023 figures already announced; there is no forward-looking KPI disclosure.
XDR license renewal and SOC retainer commercial terms are in procurement review using redlined templates; these operations-only terms are not price sensitive for NE-listed securities.
Issuer workflow checkpoints (draft→QA→NE Issuer Portal) remain in place; suspected access came via a temporary admin token for a service user “svc-esg-reporter,” now rotated.
Any MNPI arising from this event will be confined to the Sustainability and Security functions per our information barrier standard, with additions to the insider list managed by compliance.ops@horizon-oasis.example.
References: KMA Market Conduct Guide s.3 (insider trading), PDPL principles (data minimization), and our Cross-Border Transfer SOP referencing the SDAIA transfer mechanism.
Please address queries to soc@horizon-oasis.example or pdpl.office@horizon-oasis.example; external stakeholders should use our public helpline on the website rather than replying to this internal channel.
